2603.20637.title: AEGIS: From Clues to Verdicts -- Graph-Guided Deep Vulnerability Reasoning via Dialectics and Meta-Auditing
2603.20637.paper_summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly adopted for vulnerability detection, yet their reasoning remains fundamentally unsound. We identify a root cause shared by both major mitigation paradigms (agent-based debate and retrieval augmentation): reasoning in an ungrounded deliberative space that lacks a bounded, hypothesis-specific evidence base. Without such grounding, agents fabricate cross-function dependencies, and retrieval heuristics supply generic knowledge decoupled from the repository's data-flow topology. Consequently, the resulting conclusions are driven by rhetorical persuasiveness rather than verifiable facts. To ground this deliberation, we present AEGIS, a novel multi-agent framework that shifts detection from ungrounded speculation to forensic verification over a closed factual substrate. Guided by a "From Clue to Verdict" philosophy, AEGIS first identifies suspicious code anomalies (clues), then dynamically reconstructs per-variable dependency chains for each clue via on-demand slicing over a repository-level Code Property Graph. Within this closed evidence boundary, a Verifier Agent constructs competing dialectical arguments for and against exploitability, while an independent Audit Agent scrutinizes every claim against the trace, exercising veto power to prevent hallucinated verdicts. Evaluation on the rigorous PrimeVul dataset demonstrates that AEGIS establishes a new state-of-the-art, achieving 122 Pair-wise Correct Predictions. To our knowledge, this is the first approach to surpass 100 on this benchmark. It reduces the false positive rate by up to 54.40% compared to leading baselines, at an average cost of $0.09 per sample without any task-specific training.
2603.20637.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）在漏洞檢測中越來越受到採用，但它們的推理仍然根本不可靠。<lb/>我們識別出一個根本原因，這個原因在兩個主要的緩解範式（基於代理的辯論和檢索增強）中是共享的：在缺乏有界、特定假設的證據基礎的無根據的審議空間中進行推理。<lb/>沒有這樣的基礎，代理會捏造跨功能的依賴關係，而檢索啟發式則提供與資料庫的數據流拓撲脫鉤的通用知識。<lb/>因此，得出的結論是由修辭的說服力驅動，而不是可驗證的事實。<lb/>為了使這一審議有根據，我們提出了AEGIS，一個新穎的多代理框架，將檢測從無根據的推測轉變為對封閉事實基礎的法醫驗證。<lb/>根據“從線索到裁決”的理念，AEGIS首先識別可疑的代碼異常（線索），然後通過在資料庫級別的代碼屬性圖上按需切片，動態重建每個線索的每個變量依賴鏈。<lb/>在這個封閉的證據邊界內，驗證代理構建針對可利用性的競爭性辯證論點，而獨立的審計代理則對每一個主張進行審查，根據追蹤行使否決權，以防止幻覺裁決。<lb/>在嚴格的PrimeVul數據集上的評估表明，AEGIS建立了一個新的最先進的標準，實現了122對正確預測。<lb/>據我們所知，這是第一個在這一基準上超過100的方案。<lb/>與領先的基準相比，它將假陽性率降低了高達54.40%，平均每個樣本的成本為0.09美元，且不需要任何特定任務的訓練。
2603.20637.author: Sen Fang et.al.
2603.20637.authors: Sen Fang, Weiyuan Ding, Zhezhen Cao, Zhou Yang, Bowen Xu
